LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFFS SALVATORE MINEO INDIVIDUALLY, AND ON BEHALF OF ALL OTHERS SIMILARLY SITUATED, GINA SCHEMBARI INDIVIDUALLY, AND ON BEHALF OF ALL OTHER SIMILARLY SITUATED, PHILLIP NAILS, BENJAMIN DAUX AND DAVID HAYWARD'S AMENDED MOTION FOR CLASS CERTIFICATION IS GRANTED IN PART. AT THE AUGUST 30 HEARING CERTIFICATION WAS GRANTED AS TO CLASSES ONE AND TWO (FRONT DESK/INSIDE SALES EMPLOYEES AND TOUR GUIDES) AS TO UNPAID OVERTIME UNDER CALIFORNIA LAW. THIS RULING WAS INTENDED TO COVER BOTH CLAIMS UNDER BOTH THE LABOR CODE AND THE UCL, AND IS NOW RE-AFFIRMED. PLAINTIFFS SEEK CERTIFICATION FOR CLASSES ONE AND TWO FOR "MISCLASSIFICATION AS INDEPENDENT CONTRACTORS." OTHER THAN POSSIBLY ISSUES RELATING TO THE PROXIMITY OF THE TRIAL DATE, IT DOES NOT APPEAR THAT DEFENDANT OBJECTS TO SUCH REQUESTS AND THUS THESE REQUESTS ARE GRANTED WITHOUT PREJUDICE TO DEFENDANT MOVING TO CONTINUE THE TRIAL BY WAY OF MOTION OR APPLICATION MADE IN DEPARTMENT 206. THE PRINCIPAL ISSUE FOR THIS MOTION APPEARS TO BE THE ISSUE AS TO WHICH THE COURT PERMITTED SUPPLEMENTAL BRIEFING AND CONTINUED THE HEARING ON THE INITIAL MOTION FOR CLASS CERTIFICATION, I.E. WHETHER PROPOSED CLASS THREE (BUS DRIVERS) SHOULD BE CERTIFIED AS TO UNPAID OVERTIME UNDER THE UCL. DEFENDANT'S ARGUMENTS AGAINST SUCH CERTIFICATION GO TO THE MERITS OF THE CASE AND MAY BE PROPERLY ADJUDICATED BY WAY OF A MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT/ADJUDICATION, BUT NOT A MOTION FOR CLASS CERTIFICATION. SINCE THE PROPOSED CLASS THREE MEETS ALL THE REQUISITES FOR CLASS CERTIFICATION FOR ASSERTED VIOLATIONS OF THE UCL AS TO BOTH UNPAID OVERTIME AND MISCLASSIFICATION OF INDEPENDENT CONTRACTORS, CLASS THREE IS CERTIFIED FOR THESE BASES. ALL OTHER RELIEF SOUGHT IN THE INITIAL MOTION FOR CLASS CERTIFICATION IS DENIED. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN #6185.

MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES: DANIEL MARTINEZ DE LA VEGA, ESQ., 415.287.6203, FOR PLAINTIFFS SALVATORE MINEO INDIVIDUALLY, AND ON BEHALF OF ALL, GINA SCHEMBARI INDIVIDUALLY, AND ON BEHALF OF ALL, PHILLIP NAILS, BENJAMIN DAUX AND DAVID HAYWARD; No other appearances.

RULING: NO ARGUMENT. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFFS SALVATORE MINEO INDIVIDUALLY, AND ON BEHALF OF ALL OTHERS SIMILARLY SITUATED, GINA SCHEMBARI INDIVIDUALLY, AND ON BEHALF OF ALL OTHER SIMILARLY SITUATED, PHILLIP NAILS, BENJAMIN DAUX and DAVID HAYWARD'S AMENDED Motion For Class Certification IS Granted in part. At the August 30 hearing certification was granted as to classes one and two (front desk/inside sales employees and tour guides) as to unpaid overtime under California law. This ruling was intended to cover both claims under both the Labor Code and the UCL, and is now re-affirmed. Plaintiffs seek certification for classes one and two for "misclassification as independent contractors." Other than possibly issues relating to the proximity of the trial date, it does not appear that defendant objects to such requests and thus these requests are granted without prejudice to defendant moving to continue the trial by way of motion or application made in department 206. The principal issue for this motion appears to be the issue as to which the court permitted supplemental briefing and continued the hearing on the initial motion for class certification, i.e. whether proposed class three (bus drivers) should be certified as to unpaid overtime under the UCL. Defendant's arguments against such certification go to the merits of the case and may be properly adjudicated by way of a motion for summary judgment/adjudication, but not a motion for class certification. Since the proposed class three meets all the requisites for class certification for asserted violations of the UCL as to both unpaid overtime and misclassification of independent contractors, class three is certified for these bases. All other relief sought in the initial motion for class certification is denied. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Anthony Vaughn #6185.
